question_title: Will Russia impose a full (across-the-board) ban on diesel exports before July 10, 2026?
summary: As of June 28-29, 2026, Russia has NOT adopted a producer-inclusive diesel export ban — it remains officially "under consideration" with no decision and no implementation date. The Energy Ministry recommends against a full ban, and on June 28 the government instead adopted softer alternative measures (cutting the exchange-sale norm from 15% to 10%, price-step limits, import preferences), signaling a revealed preference for non-ban tools. Novak himself cited a diesel surplus, which weakens the rationale relative to the gasoline ban. The natural decision anchor (the July 31 expiry of Resolution No. 78) falls after this question's window. Offsetting these: very active high-level attention (Putin engaged, a June 29 meeting scheduled within the window) and Russia's demonstrated ability to issue such resolutions quickly (gasoline extended to producers on April 2). Net: a low but non-trivial probability that a formal producer-inclusive diesel ban is signed before July 10.

factors.description: Status quo: no producer diesel ban adopted; Putin/Novak say decision not yet taken, only under consideration; short ~10-day window.
Energy Ministry recommends against a total ban; government chose softer alternative measures on June 28 (revealed preference for non-ban tools).
Novak cited a diesel surplus (weaker rationale than gasoline); natural decision point is the July 31 expiry, after the window.
Active high-level consideration (Putin engaged, June 29 meeting); Russia can issue resolutions quickly (gasoline-to-producers precedent April 2).

reasoning:
**Forecast: 14% (NO favored)**

Russia is actively debating a full producer-inclusive diesel export ban — Novak proposed it and Putin discussed it — but as of June 28-29 no decision had been taken. Three things push strongly toward NO: (1) the government explicitly deferred and instead adopted softer measures (cutting the exchange-sale norm to 10%), (2) the Energy Ministry recommends against a ban, and (3) Novak cited a diesel *surplus*, undercutting the rationale, while the natural decision point (July 31 expiry) falls after the window. Offsetting this is genuine high-level momentum, a June 29 meeting within the window, and Russia's demonstrated ability to sign such resolutions quickly. Net probability: **0.14**.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, between June 27, 2026 and July 9, 2026 (inclusive), the Russian government formally adopts a measure (e.g., a government resolution/postanovlenie or directive) that bans the export of diesel fuel by direct producers of petroleum products, thereby extending the diesel export prohibition to all categories of exporters (producers and non-producers alike). It resolves NO otherwise. Resolution will be based on the official text published on the Russian government legal-acts portal ([publication.pravo.gov.ru](http://publication.pravo.gov.ru/)) or on reporting by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) describing the adopted measure.
fine_print: "Full/across-the-board ban" means a legally adopted prohibition (not merely a proposal, recommendation, or statement that one is "under consideration") that bars diesel-fuel exports by direct producers of petroleum products, in addition to the existing ban on non-producers. The measure must be formally adopted (signed/issued) within the window, even if its effective date falls later. A mere extension of the existing non-producer-only diesel ban, or measures targeting only gasoline or jet fuel, do not count. If credible sources conflict on whether such a producer-inclusive diesel export ban has been adopted, the question resolves based on the official text published on the Russian government portal (publication.pravo.gov.ru or government.ru). If no such measure is reported as adopted by July 9, 2026, the question resolves NO.

factors.description: Status quo as of June 28-29: no producer diesel ban adopted; Putin/Novak say 'decision not yet taken', only 'under consideration'. Short ~10-day window remaining.

Energy Ministry officially recommends AGAINST a total ban; government on June 28 instead adopted softer alternative measures (cut exchange-sale norm 15%->10%, price-step limits, import preferences) — revealed preference for non-ban tools.
Novak himself cited a diesel SURPLUS as rationale — weaker case for a full export ban than gasoline (which had a shortage); the natural decision point is the July 31 expiry, AFTER the window.

Very active high-level consideration (Novak proposal, Putin acknowledgment, June 29 meeting scheduled); Russia can issue such resolutions quickly (gasoline extended to producers April 2). Real chance of near-term adoption.

input.anchor: ~15%: base rate for a contested, ministry-opposed government measure being formally adopted within a ~10-day window while still officially 'under consideration'
input.assessment: Predictive/status-quo question with a very short window (June 27-July 9). Despite intense high-level discussion, the government has explicitly NOT decided and just opted for softer alternative measures on June 28. Energy Ministry opposes a full ban, and the diesel surplus undercuts the rationale. These push toward NO. But the topic is hot, Putin is personally engaged, a June 29 decision meeting is set, and Russia can publish resolutions fast (gasoline precedent). The natural decision anchor is July 31, after the window. Net: low but non-trivial probability of a formal producer-inclusive diesel ban being signed in the next ~10 days.
input.calibration_notes: Status quo strongly favors NO; revealed preference (alternative measures chosen June 28) is strong evidence. Avoid over-weighting newsworthy 'considering' headlines. Not hedging to 50 — committing to low teens.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the June 29 meeting produces an immediate ban resolution vs. continued deferral to July 31. Refinery-attack severity could force urgent action.
input.tool_audit: research() returned rich, directly-relevant findings with primary sources (TASS, Kommersant, government.ru). No failures.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was highly effective here — single call returned the exact decision-status snapshot needed. No friction for this fast-moving policy question.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
All four factors are well-grounded in the research trace, which returned rich primary-sourced findings (TASS, Kommersant, government.ru, pnp.ru, ren.tv, Bloomberg, Reuters).

Factor verification:
- Factor 1 (logit -1.2): "decision not yet taken / under consideration, short window" — directly supported: Novak "the decision has not yet been taken," Putin "still being considered" (June 28). ✓
- Factor 2 (logit -1.0): "Energy Ministry recommends against; June 28 softer measures (exchange-sale norm 15%→10%, price-step limits, import preferences)" — directly supported in trace. ✓
- Factor 3 (logit -0.7): "Novak cited diesel SURPLUS; July 31 expiry is natural decision point, after window" — supported (surplus rationale; Postanovlenie No. 78 runs to July 31). ✓
- Factor 4 (logit +1.0): "active high-level consideration, June 29 meeting, gasoline-to-producers precedent April 2" — supported; sign correctly positive for YES. ✓

No hallucination, no double-counting (the three NO factors use distinct evidence), no wrong-direction factor. The narrative assessment is consistent with the factor balance.

Pre-publication event check: Resolution window starts June 27, before publication (June 29), but research confirms NO ban was adopted before publication — only softer alternative measures on June 28. The agent correctly treated nothing as already-resolving and built a forward-looking estimate. No Case 2 violation.

Resolution alignment: Strong. Factors engage precisely with the producer-inclusive distinction (vs. gasoline ban, vs. mere extension of the non-producer ban), matching the criteria's specific terms.

Tool audit: Consistent with ground-truth metrics (6 calls, 0 errors). The "No such tool available: research" line in the trace was an immediate retry routed to mcp__research__research, not a counted error. Minor: the agent's audit narrative mentions only the research call and omits the 4 web_search calls, but this neither contradicts the metrics nor affects the forecast.

Counterargument is genuine and substantive (constructs the coherent YES story: Novak championing, Putin engagement, in-window June 29 meeting, fast gasoline precedent, drone-strike urgency, ready legal template) rather than a token restatement. Self-confidence 0.78 is reasonable given fresh, on-point, primary-sourced evidence with residual genuine event risk.

Anchor divergence: anchor ~15% vs. final 14% — negligible, no narrative-driven departure.

Worldview consistency: The fresh research entry matches the trace. The only other related entry (gasoline ban extension timeline) is superseded/stale and, if anything, supports the NO lean (decision/extension timing tends to cluster near the July 31 expiry, after this window). No binary/numeric inconsistency (no numeric sub-forecast on this quantity). No contradictions.

Independent probability estimate: 12–18%, driven by explicit non-decision + ministry opposition + surplus rationale + softer measures chosen, partly offset by genuine live momentum and Russia's demonstrated speed. The agent's 14% sits squarely in this range. No meaningful divergence — approve.
